userMessage: Suggest the best acquisition settings for capturing a 1MHz square wave with minimal aliasing on my MSO6B

response.text:
Built a full MSO6B flow assuming the 1 MHz square wave is on CH1 and applying a conservative anti-aliasing starting point with HIRes, 100 ns/div, 10 kpoints, FastFrame off, and a rising-edge trigger, then reading back the actual sample rate.

actionsJson.summary: Built a full MSO6B flow assuming the 1 MHz square wave is on CH1 and applying a conservative anti-aliasing starting point with HIRes, 100 ns/div, 10 kpoints, FastFrame off, and a rising-edge trigger, then reading back the actual sample rate.
suggestedFixes: Auto-repaired missing/duplicate step ids in replace_flow actions for apply compatibility.
Filled connect/disconnect instrumentIds with workspace alias "MSO6B" for direct chat apply compatibility.

flow.name: MSO6B 1 MHz Square Wave Anti-Aliasing Setup
flow.description: Recommended starting setup for capturing a 1 MHz square wave with minimal aliasing on an MSO6B. Assumes the signal is on CH1.

params.text: Assumes the 1 MHz square wave is on CH1. This setup uses HIRes, 100 ns/div, 10 kpoint record length, FastFrame off, and a rising-edge CH1 trigger as a conservative starting point to reduce aliasing while keeping a stable view.

params.command: ACQuire:MODe HIRes; HORizontal:SCAle 1.0E-7; HORizontal:RECOrdlength 10000; HORizontal:FASTframe:STATE OFF
TRIGger:A:EDGE:SOUrce CH1; TRIGger:A:EDGE:SLOpe RISe; ACQuire:STATE RUN
